steps.title: Identify what 'given that' means
steps.content: When a problem says given that the T-shirt is a Flames T-shirt, we're finding a conditional probability. The condition restricts our sample space to only Flames T-shirts. We need to calculate: out of all the Flames T-shirts in the bin, how many are medium?

steps.title: Read values from the table and calculate
steps.content: From the table, the Flames column shows 22 medium T-shirts and 44 total Flames T-shirts. So: $$P(\text{Medium | Flames}) = \frac{22}{44} = \frac{1}{2} = 0.5$$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
